Energy & Inputs.title: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $95 in July?
Energy & Inputs.why_it_matters: Near coin-flip odds (50% YES) crude reach $95. Closes in 7d. Would lift farm diesel and pass through to nitrogen fertilizer cost within a few months.

Energy & Inputs.title: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (LOW) $65 in July?
Energy & Inputs.why_it_matters: Tail-risk bet (1% odds) on crude drop below $65. Closes in 7d. A move this low would ease diesel and nitrogen input cost pressure heading into side-dress and summer ops.

Energy & Inputs.title: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $100 in July?
Energy & Inputs.why_it_matters: Lower-probability outcome (25%) that crude reach $100. Closes in 7d. Would lift farm diesel and pass through to nitrogen fertilizer cost within a few months.

Energy & Inputs.title: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (LOW) $85 in July?
Energy & Inputs.why_it_matters: Near coin-flip odds (53% YES) crude drop below $85. Closes in 7d. A move this low would ease diesel and nitrogen input cost pressure heading into side-dress and summer ops.

Energy & Inputs.title: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (HIGH) $3.10 Week of July 20 2026?
Energy & Inputs.why_it_matters: Natural gas is the primary input for nitrogen fertilizer -- price spikes raise urea costs.

Economy & Markets.title: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
Economy & Markets.why_it_matters: Fed policy moves ripple through the dollar and farm lending -- 76% YES on this outcome.

Economy & Markets.title: Will the Fed increase interest rates by 25 bps after the July 2026 meeting?
Economy & Markets.why_it_matters: Fed policy moves ripple through the dollar and farm lending -- 23% YES on this outcome.

Economy & Markets.title: Will the Fed increase interest rates by 50+ bps after the July 2026 meeting?
Economy & Markets.why_it_matters: Fed policy moves ripple through the dollar and farm lending -- 1% YES on this outcome.

Economy & Markets.title: Will no Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
Economy & Markets.why_it_matters: 84% odds of a rate cut. Lower rates reduce operating loan and land interest carrying cost -- historically supportive of commodity prices via weaker dollar.
